assumptions: bullish + bearish will never exceed total (guaranteed by neutral = total - bullish - bearish counting logic)
Callers of compute_market_sentiment() accept neutral_pct derived as residual; no caller requires all three to be independently computed
Empty-list early-return path is unaffected — returns hardcoded 0.0s before the new variable assignments
unresolved_risks: If future code allows bullish or bearish counts to exceed total (a separate bug), neutral_pct would become negative. This risk pre-existed the fix and is not introduced by it.

Python float subtraction 100.0 - 33.3 - 33.3 produces a small epsilon; round(..., 1) normalises correctly but extreme accumulation could theoretically drift for very high counts — not observed in tested range.

claims.claim: Empty ticker list returns market_score=0.0 and all percentages 0.0

summary: Applied residual-derivation fix to compute_market_sentiment() in api/services/sentiment_aggregator.py. All 5 test assertions pass. Single file changed, 4 lines removed, 8 lines added. No new dependencies introduced.
